# Raino Studio — Agentic PCB Design Platform

## Overview

Raino Studio is the product application for Raino, an agentic PCB and PCBA workflow platform. It requires authentication via Supabase magic link. Once signed in, users can create hardware projects and step through the full design workflow: from a natural language description of what they want to build, through specification, architecture selection, part sourcing, BOM generation, KiCad project creation, and rough cost quoting.

Raino Studio is not a chatbot. Every design decision is bounded by approved architecture templates. Every part selection is traceable to its source. Every quote marks which prices came from live supplier data and which came from fixture estimates. The system stops and asks for input when it hits ambiguity it cannot resolve on its own.


## Public Routes

/login — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/login
Magic link sign-in. Users enter their email and receive a login link.

/signup — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/signup
Account creation. Collects email and creates a Supabase auth account.


## Authenticated Routes

All routes below require a valid session. Unauthenticated users are redirected to the login page.

/ (Dashboard) — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/
Project list and management. Shows all projects owned by the authenticated user. Each project card displays its name, current workflow step, and last updated timestamp.

/projects/new — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/projects/new
Create a new project. The user provides a project name and an initial natural language description of the hardware they want to build.

/projects/{id} — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/projects/{id}
Project detail page. Shows the project overview and workflow progress. Contains sub-pages for each workflow step.

/projects/{id}/intake — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/projects/{id}/intake
Natural language intake. The user describes their hardware intent in plain English.

/projects/{id}/spec — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/projects/{id}/spec
Structured specification. Displays the compiled spec with validated fields: target application, power requirements, interfaces, size constraints, environmental conditions, compliance needs, and cost targets.

/projects/{id}/architecture — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/projects/{id}/architecture
Architecture template selection. Shows the approved architecture template that matches the project requirements.

/projects/{id}/bom — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/projects/{id}/bom
Bill of materials. Displays the full BOM with supplier links, pricing, stock levels, MOQ requirements, and alternate parts for every line item.

/projects/{id}/quote — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/projects/{id}/quote
Rough cost quote. Shows the quote breakdown with confidence band (high, medium, or low), subtotal calculation, and a full list of assumptions.

/projects/{id}/previews — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/projects/{id}/previews
Design previews. Shows generated schematic and layout previews when available.

/projects/{id}/downloads — https://raino-studio.vercel.app/projects/{id}/downloads
Artifact downloads. Provides download links for Gerber files, BOM exports, and manufacturing handoff bundles.


## API Routes

All project API routes require authentication and enforce ownership via verifyProjectOwnership. Unauthenticated requests receive a 401 response. Requests from authenticated users who do not own the project receive a 404 response (not 403, to avoid leaking project existence).

API routes handle:
- Project CRUD operations
- Workflow step transitions
- Intake and clarification state
- Spec read/write
- Architecture selection
- BOM generation and retrieval
- Quote calculation and retrieval
- File upload and download for design artifacts


## The Workflow

Raino Studio guides each project through ten main workflow steps:

1. Intake — The user describes what they want to build in natural language.

2. Clarification — The LLM asks follow-up questions to resolve ambiguity. This loop continues until the system has enough information. It will not proceed past critical uncertainty without user input.

3. Spec compilation — The system compiles the user's answers into a structured specification with validated fields.

4. Architecture selection — The system selects an approved architecture template that matches the requirements. Architecture templates are predefined, audited designs that constrain the solution space.

5. Ingestion — Datasheets, errata, and application notes for selected parts are ingested through an 8-stage pipeline with sufficiency gates. If critical documents are missing, the system flags the gap.

6. BOM generation — A KiCad-ready bill of materials is assembled with alternate parts for every line item. Each part includes supplier links, pricing, stock, and MOQ.

7. Quote generation — A rough cost quote is produced with three confidence bands. High: all BOM lines have real supplier prices. Medium: 70%+ real, 30% or fewer estimates. Low: more than 30% estimates. Every quote includes a full assumption list.

8. Design generation — KiCad schematic and layout files are produced via the KiCad CLI. KiCad is an external GPL-licensed tool invoked via CLI commands only. If the KiCad CLI is unavailable, this step is skipped and labeled.

9. Preview and download — The user can preview generated designs and download artifacts including Gerber files, BOM exports, and manufacturing handoff bundles.

10. Handoff — A manufacturing handoff bundle is assembled with all deliverables for PCBA fabrication.


## Authentication and Security

Auth uses Supabase magic link (passwordless email login). The middleware refreshes session cookies on every request.

Project data is isolated by ownership. Users can only access their own projects. The verifyProjectOwnership function checks both authentication status and project ownership on every API request.

Supabase Row-Level Security (RLS) provides an additional layer of data isolation at the database level.


## Degraded Mode

Raino Studio runs in degraded mode when credentials are missing. Every degraded path is clearly labeled.

Without Supabase credentials: the app shows auth prompts but cannot persist projects or run RAG queries. Static UI still renders.

Without KIMI_API_KEY: the app works except LLM calls. Natural language intake and reasoning fall back to manual input modes.

Without supplier API keys (DigiKey, Mouser, JLCPCB): the full workflow runs, but pricing, stock, and MOQ data come from labeled fixture data. Quotes are marked as estimates.

Without KiCad CLI: project management, BOM, and quote steps work normally. KiCad project generation is skipped with a clear label.

Mock adapters and fixture data are permanent parts of the codebase. They are not temporary hacks. They exist so the system is always inspectable and testable.


## No-Fake-Integration Policy

Raino Studio never fabricates live pricing or stock data. It never claims a live API connection when using fixture data. It never silently downgrades to degraded mode without reporting it. And it never proceeds past critical ambiguity without user confirmation.

Every degraded-mode path is inspectable through the audit trail. Mock adapters are permanent, honest fallbacks.


## Tech Stack

Frontend: Next.js 15, React 19, Tailwind CSS v4
Design: @raino/ui pixel-art cyberpunk design system
Backend: Next.js Route Handlers, Server Actions, Supabase
Database: Supabase Postgres, Prisma ORM, pgvector
Auth: Supabase Auth (magic link)
Storage: Supabase Storage
LLM: Kimi K2.5 via OpenAI SDK (moonshot.ai)
Validation: Zod
Testing: Vitest
Deploy: Vercel


## Marketing Site

The public-facing marketing site with documentation, feature descriptions, architecture overview, and workflow explanation is at:

https://raino-site.vercel.app/


## License

Raino Studio is MIT licensed. KiCad is an external GPL-licensed tool and is not embedded in the Raino codebase.
